name: Harper Road Technology Park
info: Proposed $1.5B multi-building campus in Peculiar (Cass County). Surfaced via opposition tracker: city added data-center zoning definition enabling the project; organized resident opposition followed (2024-10 zoning fight). Coordinates approximate (city centroid). Sources: opposition tracker records; harborcountry/local coverage pending.

name: Province Group Perry Village Data Center Campus
info: Seven-building campus on 163 acres; developer had agreed to purchase land from Perry Village for $8.4M. Zoning application formally withdrawn July 2025 per opposition tracker records. Announced date not verified; coordinates approximate (village centroid).

name: Meta Hyperion
info: Meta's multi-billion-dollar Richland Parish AI campus announced December 2024; under construction. Reported multi-GW scale; capacity left blank pending sourced figure. Associated federal scrutiny (Senate EPW inquiry over Entergy gas plants) tracked in opposition records. Coordinates approximate.

name: Prado AI Industrial Campus
companies: Prado AI; PraCon Global Investment Group
info: Proposed AI data center + semiconductor fabrication campus with dedicated 350 MW gas plant (plant capacity, not IT load). PSC declaratory-opinion request filed 2026-03-02; PSC declined to opine 2026-05-29 calling request premature; developer says project proceeding, site now described as Jackson metro, undisclosed. Sources: Mississippi Today 2026-04-22 and 2026-06-01; WLBT 2026-05-29.

name: New Carlisle Chicago Trail Data Center (Third Site)
address: Chicago Trail between Spruce Rd and Walnut Rd
info: ~$12-13B proposal on 1,057 acres of farmland; developer undisclosed (petition entity tied to 80 Broad St NY address; land owned by Mall-O-Manor LLC / Szczypiorski family). Rezoning DENIED 7-2 by St. Joseph County Council 2025-12-09 after unfavorable Area Plan Commission and Land Use Planning Committee recommendations; renewed petition activity first-read 2026-05-13 per tracker. Phase reflects active petition; the 2025-12-09 denial is a sourced adverse milestone. Sources: South Bend Tribune / WSBT / WNDU Dec 2025; DCD May 2026.

name: Project Maize / Google Michigan City Data Center
info: $832M conversion of former Federal Mogul facility (400,000 sq ft brownfield). Plans submitted to Common Council early 2025 (date precision: year); council approved four resolutions Sept 2025; construction began fall 2025; Google confirmed acquisition 2026-04-16. Opposition: IUOE Local 150 labor protests and picketing. Sources: WNDU/WSBT 2025-2026, Building Indiana Sept 2025.

name: Meta Project Everest
info: Second Meta data center campus in Richland Parish, separate from Hyperion. Surfaced via LPSC filing: Meta subsidiary Evest LLC applied 2026-03-25 for approval of generation/transmission resources tied to a 20-year contract (Docket per tracker record). Exact site undisclosed; coordinates are parish-approximate. Source: opposition tracker LPSC record.

name: Terra Nexus Custer Avenue Data Center
info: 200 MW data center; plans submitted to Fayetteville fall 2025 (date precision: month approximated to Oct). Sited near the Fayetteville PWC power plant. Coordinates approximate. Source: CityView NC 2026-05-22.

name: PRSM Group Cumberland County Data Center
info: PRSM Group (Charlotte/Arlington VA) client project: two 1M-sq-ft phases, 225 MW, on 50 acres in an undisclosed Cumberland County industrial park. Publicly disclosed by partner Eric Little at the county's 2026-05-18 moratorium hearing, where he argued against the moratorium; county adopted a 6-month moratorium for unincorporated areas 2026-06-15. Source: CityView NC 2026-05-22 and 2026-06-16.

name: Project Sail Data Center
address: Wagers Mill Road / Welcome Sargent Road
info: Hyperscale campus, 9 buildings ~4.34M sq ft, ~900 MW, on ~829 acres near Newnan. Atlas Development first filed rezoning December 2024 (rural conservation to industrial); amended January 2026 after county adopted its data center ordinance Dec 2025. Coweta County Board of Commissioners approved the rezoning 3-2 on 2026-04-07. Prologis agreed to buy the site May 2025. 17 residents filed a Superior Court appeal 2026-05-05 seeking to void the rezoning — finality watch. Coordinates approximate. Sources: Atlanta News First 2026-04-08, The Citizen 2026-04-10, DeSmog 2026-04-07, CBS Atlanta 2026-05-07.

name: Howell Township Data Center (Project Splitrock)
info: ~1,077-acre conditional rezoning (agricultural to Research & Technology) for a $1B single-story data-processing campus by Randee LLC / Stantec, early-stage codename 'Project Splitrock'; a township trustee alleged Meta as the end client (unconfirmed). Announced/filed for the 2025-09-23 Planning Commission hearing. Howell Township Planning Commission voted unanimously against rezoning 2025-09-23; Livingston County Planning Commission unanimously against 2025-11-19; township enacted a 6-month moratorium 2025-11-20 (worded to exempt this request). Developer WITHDREW the rezoning request and zoning text amendment ahead of the 2025-12-08 township board meeting. Outcome blocked_confirmed via withdrawal (not a board denial vote). Coordinates approximate. Sources: Planet Detroit 2025-12-08, FracTracker 2026-01, DCD 2026-03-31, Michigan Advance 2025-11-21.

name: Natelli Vance County Data Center
address: US-158 Business, Triangle North Vance, near Henderson
info: Rezoning of ~113 acres (three parcels) from Employment & Institutional to Light Industrial along US-158 Business, part of a larger ~619-acre corridor; no end user named, widely expected to be a data center (same developer as the withdrawn Apex/New Hill project). Faced organized opposition (moratorium petition, ~150 residents at the April 6 hearing). Vance County Planning Board approved unanimously in March 2026; Board of Commissioners delayed at the April 6 meeting, then approved 6-1 at a special-called meeting on 2026-04-20. Coordinates approximate. Sources: WRAL 2026-04-20, Henderson Dispatch 2026-06-06, DCD 2026-05-07.
